content.problem_latex:
Ms. Osborne asks each student in her class to draw a rectangle with integral side lengths and a perimeter of $$50$$ units. All of her students calculate the area of the rectangle they draw. What is the difference between the largest and smallest possible areas of the rectangles?
(A) 76
(B) 120
(C) 128
(D) 132
(E) 136
content.problem_plain: Ms. Osborne asks each student in her class to draw a rectangle with integral side lengths and a perimeter of 50 units. All of her students calculate the area of the rectangle they draw. What is the difference between the largest and smallest possible areas of the rectangles? (A) 76 (B) 120 (C) 128 (D) 132 (E) 136
content.solution_latex: As we know, the sum of the length and width is $$25$$. The largest area is $$13\times12=156$$ and the smallest area is $$24\times1=24$$, so the difference is $$156-24=132$$.
content.solution_plain: As we know, the sum of the length and width is 25 . The largest area is 13 12=156 and the smallest area is 24 1=24 , so the difference is 156-24=132 .

step_outline: As we know, the sum of the length and width is $$25$$.
The largest area is $$13\times12=156$$ and the smallest area is $$24\times1=24$$, so the difference is $$156-24=132$$.

search.solution_text: As we know, the sum of the length and width is 25 . The largest area is 13 12=156 and the smallest area is 24 1=24 , so the difference is 156-24=132 .

content.problem_plain: Farmer Dunphy has 100 metres of fencing. He wants to make a closed rectangular pen. He uses a long wall for one of the sides. Each side of the pen is a whole number in metres. What is the largest area that the pen can be? (A) 125 m ^ 2 (B) 625 m ^ 2 (C) 1000 m ^ 2 (D) 1250 m ^ 2 (E) 2500 m ^ 2
content.solution_latex: $a+2b=100$, when $$a=2b=50$$, the largest area is $$50\times (50\div 2)=1250$$ m\textsuperscript{2}.
content.solution_plain: a+2b=100 , when a=2b=50 , the largest area is 50 (50 2)=1250 m 2 .

step_outline: $a+2b=100$, when $$a=2b=50$$, the largest area is $$50\times (50\div 2)=1250$$ m\textsuperscript{2}.

content.problem_plain: Farmer Dunphy has 16 metres of fencing. He wants to make a closed rectangular pen. He uses a long wall for one of the sides. Each side of the pen is a whole number in metres. What is the largest area that the pen can be? (A) 25 m ^ 2 (B) 27 m ^ 2 (C) 30 m ^ 2 (D) 28 m ^ 2 (E) 32 m ^ 2
content.solution_latex: $a+2b=16$, when $$a=2b=8$$, the largest area is $$8\times (8\div 2)=32$$ m\textsuperscript{2}.
content.solution_plain: a+2b=16 , when a=2b=8 , the largest area is 8 (8 2)=32 m 2 .

step_outline: $a+2b=16$, when $$a=2b=8$$, the largest area is $$8\times (8\div 2)=32$$ m\textsuperscript{2}.

content.problem_latex:
The cost of my algebra book is $$200\textbackslash\%$$ of that of my geometry book.  The cost of my geometry book is $$\frac{4}{3}$$ that of my calculus book. If my calculus book costs ＄$$21$$, how much do all $$3$$ books cost together?
(A) ＄$$28
(B) ＄$$56
(C) ＄$$84
(D) ＄$$105
content.problem_plain: The cost of my algebra book is 200 % of that of my geometry book. The cost of my geometry book is 4 3 that of my calculus book. If my calculus book costs ＄ 21 , how much do all 3 books cost together? (A) ＄ 28 (B) ＄ 56 (C) ＄ 84 (D) ＄ 105
content.solution_latex: The cost of the calculus book is ＄$$21$$. The cost of my geometry book is ＄$$21\times \left(\frac{4}{3}\right)=$$＄$$28$$. My algebra book costs ＄$$28 \times2 =$$＄$$56$$. All $$3$$ books cost ＄$$21+$$＄$$28 +$$＄$$56 =$$＄$$105$$ in total.
content.solution_plain: The cost of the calculus book is ＄ 21 . The cost of my geometry book is ＄ 21 ( 4 3 )= ＄ 28 . My algebra book costs ＄ 28 2 = ＄ 56 . All 3 books cost ＄ 21+ ＄ 28 + ＄ 56 = ＄ 105 in total.

step_outline: The cost of the calculus book is ＄$$21$$.
The cost of my geometry book is ＄$$21\times \left(\frac{4}{3}\right)=$$＄$$28$$.

content.problem_latex:
Algebra books cost $$$12.50$$ each, and geometry books cost $$$14$$ each. How much do $$6$$ algebra books and $$5$$ geometry books cost in total?
(A) 145.00
(B) 146.50
(C) 150.00
(D) 151.50
content.problem_plain: Algebra books cost 12.50 each, and geometry books cost 14 each. How much do 6 algebra books and 5 geometry books cost in total? (A) 145.00 (B) 146.50 (C) 150.00 (D) 151.50
content.solution_latex: Six algebra books cost $$$12.50 \times 6 = $75$$. Five geometry books cost $$$14\times5 = $70$$. All together, they cost a total of $$$145$$.
content.solution_plain: Six algebra books cost 12.50 6 = 75 . Five geometry books cost 14 5 = 70 . All together, they cost a total of 145 .

step_outline: All together, they cost a total of $$$145$$.
